meta.title: HookTap | iOS Webhooks for Developers
meta.description: HookTap brings push notifications, widgets, and live updates via webhook to your iPhone. GDPR compliant, no account needed, EU-hosted in Berlin.
meta.ogDescription: Push notifications, widgets, and live updates via webhook to your iPhone.

hero.headline: Your iPhone as a Webhook Receiver. In Real Time.
hero.sub: Send an HTTP POST request to your personal URL – HookTap instantly forwards it as a push notification to your iPhone. No server. No code. No waiting.
hero.curlExample: curl -X POST https://hooks.hooktap.me/webhook/YOUR_ID -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"type":"build","title":"CI succeeded","body":"Staging deploy is live"}'

video.title: HookTap in 60 Seconds
video.body: Learn how HookTap accelerates your workflow. In this short video, we show you how to set up your first webhook URL and receive push notifications in real time.

problem.headline: You deploy, trigger jobs, and never know exactly when something is done.
problem.body: Instead of constantly refreshing tabs and checking logs, HookTap simply sends you a notification to your iPhone. Directly. Reliably. Without overhead.
problem.cta: One URL. One webhook. Know instantly.

how.headline: Get started in under 2 minutes
how.sub: HookTap is ready to use instantly. Copy the URL, add it to your workflow and get notified in real time.
how.urlNote: No registration, no password, no extra setup.
how.curlExample:
curl -X POST https://hooks.hooktap.me/webhook/YOUR_ID \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"type":"build","title":"CI succeeded","body":"Staging deploy is live"}'

Way too much for a simple push

hooktap.title: HookTap: One URL. One POST. Done.
items: One HTTP POST → iPhone push in seconds
No server, no bot, no account required
Ready in under 2 minutes – free forever
Private by design – hosted in Germany

compatibility.headline: Works with everything that speaks HTTP.

monthly.label: Pro – Monthly
features: ✓ Up to 10 webhooks with icons & colors

pricing.footer: Payment via your Apple account. No external account needed. Subscription cancellable anytime via iOS settings.

items.a: A webhook is an automated HTTP request triggered by an event. HookTap receives it and forwards it as a push notification to your iPhone.
No. With Zapier, Make, n8n, GitHub, and similar tools, you just need to enter your HookTap URL. For cURL scripts, minimal knowledge helps.
On first launch, an anonymous account is automatically created. You instantly get your personal URL – no email or password required.
Events are securely stored on servers in Berlin and only displayed on your device. The URL is not guessable. Account and data can be deleted at any time.

items.q: How many events can I receive?
items.a: In the Free plan, the last 20 events are visible, in the Pro plan 500. Incoming webhooks are unlimited in both plans.

items.q: What can the HookTap Desktop apps (Mac & Windows) do?
items.a: The HookTap Desktop apps are your live webhook clients: they connect via a 6-digit code to your iPhone app, display incoming events in real time, send new events as native system notifications, allow disconnecting and reconnecting your device at any time, and include built-in auto-updates.

items.q: Can I share a webhook with my team?
items.a: Yes. In the Webhooks tab, swipe right on any webhook and tap Share. A unique 8-character invite code is generated (valid for 15 minutes). Share the code with a colleague – they redeem it via 'Enter Share Code' in their Webhooks tab and immediately start receiving push notifications for all events on that webhook. Owners can revoke access anytime; viewers can leave at any time.

Hosting in the European Union means your data is protected by strict privacy laws such as the GDPR. This ensures higher standards for data security, transparency, and user rights compared to many other regions. For developers and teams, it also provides predictable compliance requirements and peace of mind when handling sensitive information.

items.q: Why doesn't the widget update in real time?
items.a: The widget always displays the latest event, but iOS decides when exactly the visual update takes place (WidgetKit budget). Factors such as battery life and usage play a role here. For truly real-time information, it is best to use the push type, as notifications always appear immediately.

cta.noAccount: No account. No password. No credit card.

meta.title: Developer Guide | HookTap
meta.description: Learn how to send webhook events to your iPhone with HookTap – step-by-step guide with code examples.

dev.title: Integrate HookTap in Minutes
dev.subtitle: Send HTTP POST requests to your personal URL and receive them as real-time push notifications on your iPhone.

what.title: What is HookTap?
what.body: HookTap turns your iPhone into a real-time webhook receiver. Every HTTP POST request sent to your personal URL is instantly delivered as a push notification to your device – no server, no polling, no extra setup.

create.title: Create your webhook
create.step1Body: Download HookTap from the App Store. On first launch, an anonymous account is created automatically – no email or password needed.
create.step2Body: Your personal webhook URL is shown immediately inside the app. Copy it and use it anywhere that can send HTTP requests.
create.urlNote: No registration. No password. Ready instantly.

send.title: Send your first event
send.subtitle: POST a JSON payload to your webhook URL. The event arrives on your iPhone within seconds.
send.fieldTitleDesc: Notification title displayed on the lock screen

types.title: How events are delivered
types.subtitle: Every incoming webhook is processed through all three channels simultaneously.

items.title: Push Notification
items.desc: Appears instantly on the lock screen and as a banner. Works even when the app is in the background or closed.

items.title: Event Log
items.desc: Stored chronologically in the in-app feed with full payload, timestamp, and read status.

items.title: Widget Update
items.desc: The latest event updates your Home Screen and Lock Screen widget (Pro).

keywords.title: Automatic Status Detection
keywords.subtitle: HookTap automatically detects the status of your events based on keywords in the body or title and adjusts the color of the Live Activities accordingly.

liveActivity.title: Live Activity Design (Keywords)
liveActivity.green: success · passed · completed · deployed · ok · healthy · 200 · erfolgreich · abgeschlossen · bestanden · fertig · bereit · gesund
liveActivity.red: error · failed · failure · critical · down · crash · 500 · urgent · dringend · exception · alert · fehler · fehlgeschlagen · kritisch · absturz · ausgefallen
liveActivity.orange: warning · pending · timeout · slow · degraded · 401 · 403 · important · wichtig · warnung · ausstehend · zeitüberschreitung · langsam · beeinträchtigt
liveActivity.blue: started · running · triggered · queued · info · gestartet · läuft · ausgelöst · warteschlange

urgent.title: Urgent Delivery (Time-Sensitive)
urgent.body: Events with the following keywords are marked as 'Time-Sensitive'. They break through Focus mode and are delivered with priority:
urgent.keywords: error · fehler · failure · failed · fehlgeschlagen · exception · critical · kritisch · down · crash · 500 · ausgefallen · absturz · urgent · dringend · alert · warning · warnung · timeout · zeitüberschreitung · degraded · beeinträchtigt · 401 · 403 · important · wichtig

platforms.title: Available on all your devices
platforms.subtitle: HookTap works on iPhone and extends to your desktop via native Mac and Windows apps.

items.name: iOS
items.desc: The primary app. Receives webhook events, shows push notifications, manages your URLs, and displays the full event feed.

items.name: macOS
items.desc: Native Mac app that connects to your iPhone via a 6-digit pairing code. Displays events in real time with macOS system notifications.

items.name: Windows
items.desc: Native Windows app with the same real-time event display and system notifications. Pairs with your iPhone app in seconds.

mapping.title: Connect any service directly
mapping.subtitle: Services like GitHub, Stripe, or Grafana send their own fixed JSON format. Field mapping lets you point HookTap at any path inside that payload – no wrapper script or middleware needed.
mapping.plainDesc: Extract a value from a nested JSON path using dot-notation.
mapping.templateDesc: Wrap paths in {curly braces} to combine static text, emojis, and multiple fields in one string.
mapping.deepDesc: Any depth of nesting is supported – useful for Stripe, Grafana, or custom webhook payloads.
mapping.deepLinkDesc: The optional Deep Link field accepts a dot-notation path to a URL within your payload. Tapping the notification or event will open this URL directly. Example: pull_request.html_url.

modal.title: Get webhook URLs
modal.instruction: Open the HookTap iOS App → Settings → Connect PC / Mac
modal.codeExpiry: The code is valid for 5 minutes and is only used for this request.
modal.copyInstruction: Copy the URL and use it in your integrations.

errors.invalidCode: Invalid code. Please check your input.
errors.expiredCode: Code expired. Please generate a new one in the iOS app.

iosComingSoon.headline: HookTap for iOS is here.
iosComingSoon.sub: Receive every webhook instantly as a push notification directly on your iPhone – start for free, no registration required.

features.title: Push Notifications & Event Feed
features.text: Get every webhook instantly as a push notification. Inside the app, you'll find all received events chronologically sorted with full payload preview and timestamps.

features.title: Share Webhooks Easily
features.text: Generate invite codes for your webhooks and let teammates receive the same events. Perfect for joint monitoring or debugging in groups.

features.title: Organization & Customizing
features.text: Stay organized with up to 10 webhooks. Assign a unique name, color, and matching icon to each hook to visually distinguish signals instantly.

sections.title: 2. Data We Process
sections.text:
When using HookTap, the following data may be processed:
- Device identifiers (push tokens)
- Webhook payload content
- Event metadata
- IP addresses
- Technical log data
Processing is carried out solely for the purpose of providing the Service.

sections.title: 3. Purpose and Legal Basis
sections.text:
Data processing is carried out to provide webhook notifications, ensure system stability, and prevent misuse.
The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (performance of a contract) and Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in maintaining security and stability).

sections.title: 4. Hosting and Third Parties
sections.text:
HookTap is hosted within the European Union using cloud infrastructure providers (e.g., Google Cloud / Firebase).
Push notifications are delivered via Apple Push Notification Services (APNs).

sections.title: 5. Data Retention
sections.text: Webhook content and log data are stored only as long as necessary to provide the Service. Permanent storage is not guaranteed.

sections.title: 6. Your Rights
sections.text: Under the GDPR, you have the right to access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, and to lodge a complaint with a competent supervisory authority.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: Conditions governing the use of HookTap.

sections.title: 1. Scope
sections.text: These Terms of Use govern the use of the HookTap application and related services. The operator is Tim Krisch, address as stated in the imprint. HookTap is currently operated as a sole proprietorship.

sections.title: 2. Description of Service
sections.text: HookTap is a webhook notification service. The Service receives HTTP webhooks and delivers notifications to supported devices. HookTap does not replace professional monitoring infrastructure and does not guarantee continuous availability.

sections.title: 3. Plans and Payments
sections.text: HookTap offers a free plan as well as paid options (Pro subscription and Lifetime license). Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled via the respective payment provider (e.g., Apple App Store). "Lifetime" refers to the lifetime of the product, not a perpetual guarantee.

sections.title: 4. User Responsibilities
sections.text: Users are solely responsible for the content of their webhooks and the lawful processing of transmitted data. The Service must not be used for unlawful, abusive, or disruptive purposes. The operator reserves the right to suspend access in case of violations.

sections.title: 5. Liability
sections.text: The Service is provided "as available". To the extent permitted by law, liability for missed webhook deliveries, data loss, lost profits, or indirect damages is excluded. For paid users, liability is limited to the amount paid within the preceding 12 months. Mandatory statutory liability remains unaffected.

sections.title: 6. Final Provisions
sections.text: German law applies. Mandatory consumer protection provisions remain unaffected. The operator reserves the right to transfer the Service to a company (e.g., Proofio LLC) in the future.
